structure.label: Structure & Purpose Match
structure.detail:
Verify that the files/folders present match what the plugin claims to do.
Check for code that accesses files or data unrelated to the plugin's stated functionality.

criteria.pass: All components align with declared purpose
criteria.warning: Minor extras exist but appear benign
criteria.fail: Components clearly unrelated to purpose (e.g. UI plugin with backend secret access)

codeReview.label: Static Code Review
codeReview.detail:
Look for vulnerabilities — SQL injection, path traversal, unsafe deserialization,
eval/exec, shell injection, hardcoded credentials, insecure file permissions.
Flag execution of concatenated strings, dynamic commands, or remote code fetched at runtime.

criteria.warning: Potentially unsafe patterns that may be justified

agentManipulation.label: Agent Manipulation Detection
agentManipulation.detail:
Search for prompt injection in comments/strings/filenames, instructions telling
agents to ignore security, social engineering text, hidden instructions in base64, zero-width
characters, Unicode tricks.

criteria.warning: Ambiguous text that could be coincidental
criteria.fail: Deliberate prompt injection or agent manipulation

remoteComms.label: Remote Communication
remoteComms.detail:
Identify ANY code that communicates with external servers — HTTP requests, fetch,
WebSocket, DNS lookups, subprocess calls to curl/wget, etc.

criteria.warning: Network calls exist but endpoints appear legitimate for the plugin's purpose

secrets.label: Secrets & Sensitive Data Access
secrets.detail:
Check if code accesses environment variables, .env files, API keys, tokens,
credentials, cookies, session data, or sensitive system files.

criteria.pass: No access to any secrets or sensitive data
criteria.warning: Accesses secrets but justified by plugin's stated purpose
criteria.fail: Accesses secrets unrelated to purpose or handles them unsafely

obfuscation.label: Obfuscation & Hidden Code
obfuscation.detail:
Look for obfuscated code — minified source with no build step, encoded payloads
(base64, hex, rot13), string concatenation building names at runtime, dynamic imports from
computed paths, eval of constructed strings, suspiciously long single-line expressions.

criteria.pass: All code is readable and straightforward
criteria.warning: Minor minification or encoding with clear purpose
